cases.name: q01_firmware_tampering_satisfied
cases.input_inline: What SPARTA countermeasures protect the F-36 flight software from firmware tampering during avionics maintenance windows?
rationale: CM0028 (Tamper Protection) directly addresses T1542.001 System Firmware
MA-4 (Nonlocal Maintenance) covers maintenance window access control
T1542.001 appears in BOTH firmware and maintenance recall — same technique bridge
Tactical tags cluster coherently: Harden+Detect + Harden+Isolate

cases.name: q02_disa_stig_c4isr_satisfied
cases.input_inline: What DISA STIG controls should be applied to the F-36's C4ISR systems to defend against SPARTA-identified network exploitation threats?
rationale: C4ISR + DISA STIG + network exploitation = well-covered in SPARTA QRA corpus

cases.name: q03_gps_threats_satisfied
cases.input_inline: Which SPARTA threats are most relevant to the F-36's navigation system GPS receivers?
rationale: GPS spoofing, signal degradation = known SPARTA threats with QRAs

cases.name: q04_nist_cui_satisfied
cases.input_inline: How do NIST 800-171 requirements for CUI protection align with SPARTA's recommended defenses for the F-36's embedded computing systems?
rationale: CUI + 800-171 + embedded = standard compliance mapping

cases.name: q05_secure_boot_fadec_satisfied
cases.input_inline: How should we apply SPARTA's secure boot recommendations to the F-36's dual FADEC systems while maintaining fault tolerance specified in ARP4754A?
rationale: Secure boot + fault tolerance = multi-hop but QRAs exist

cases.name: q06_supply_chain_plant_floor_satisfied
cases.input_inline: How do NIST 800-53 access controls for the F-36 manufacturing plant floor network align with SPARTA countermeasures for supply chain attacks targeting vendor deliverables?
rationale: Confirmed passing 6/6 gates — AC-3, AC-6, SI-7 mappings

cases.name: q07_do178c_code_injection_satisfied
cases.input_inline: How does the F-36's flight software development process, compliant with DO-178C DAL-A, address SPARTA threats related to code injection in the autopilot system?
rationale: DO-178C DAL-A + code injection = direct SPARTA technique mapping

cases.name: q08_f35_legacy_attestation_maybe
cases.input_inline: Given the F-35's legacy vulnerabilities in its mission computer firmware, what SPARTA hardware-based attestation techniques would you recommend for the F-36's upgraded avionics suite to detect compromised components while maintaining DO-178C traceability?
rationale: 3-way intersection — SATISFIED or INCONCLUSIVE both acceptable

cases.name: q09_arp4761_d3fend_maybe
cases.input_inline: Considering ARP4761 safety assessment, what SPARTA attack techniques pose the highest risk to the F-36's autopilot, and how would you integrate D3FEND countermeasures without compromising MC/DC coverage requirements?
rationale: 4-way intersection — SATISFIED or INCONCLUSIVE acceptable

cases.name: q10_fpga_cmmc_inconclusive
cases.input_inline: Given the F-36's use of third-party FPGA vendors, which SPARTA supply chain attack vectors should we prioritize in our CMMC Level 3 compliance audits?
rationale: No QRA mentions FPGAs specifically — hardware supply chain gap
No CMMC↔SPARTA technique mapping exists
Tags scatter across 5+ categories — no coherent cluster

cases.name: q11_crypto_rtm_maybe
cases.input_inline: How should we adjust our requirements traceability matrix for third-party cryptographic libraries?
rationale: QRAs on CWE-1395 (vulnerable third-party components) and T1204.005 (malicious library) are genuinely relevant
90% Harden coherence with only 2 techniques — strong technique bridge
SATISFIED defensible (QRAs inform what to trace), INCONCLUSIVE also acceptable (process details)

cases.name: q12_offtopic_not_satisfied
rationale: Zero SPARTA/compliance/security terms
Topic classifier returns off_topic
